notes: weather-stats.com/climate-data.org city comparison — nearly double Heraklion's ~464mm. Real, sourced mechanism behind the mold/damp complaint in unheated older stone buildings

value_raw: An EU-funded programme formally studied why Heraklion is *not* attractive to digital nomads — named inadequate transport infrastructure, lack of services, insufficient digitalization, limited accommodation options as the actual reasons
notes: A real, sourced official negative, not an inference from silence. Response: "Digital Nomads Heraklion" (digitalnomads.heraklion.gr) — evidence the gap was real enough to need fixing, not evidence it's fixed

fact_label: Visa & legal pathway ease — scorecard score
notes: Same national framework as Chania; the residence-permit office being physically in Heraklion is a small convenience, not enough to move the score above Chania's

value_raw: Yes
notes: Continued freeze on new Airbnb-style registration permits (AMA numbers) in central Athens through end of 2026, expanded to 5 additional areas including Chania (plus Thessaloniki, Santorini, Paros, Halkidiki). Platforms must report to the Greek tax authority (AADE) from May 2026; fines €5,000-20,000 for non-compliance

fact_label: AFM (tax ID) as prerequisite for ordinary transactions
value_raw: Required for banking, rental contracts, utilities, phone contracts, virtually every official transaction — can be obtained before arrival via a tax representative
notes: Described repeatedly as the real "skeleton key" to functioning in Greece

value_raw: A separate permit requirement from the prefect/Decentralized Administration, applying to everyone (not just non-EU foreigners), tied to specific statute-enumerated place-names
notes: Newly found 2026-07-08. Primary toponym table not independently confirmed — an indirect reconstruction surfaced plausible Chania-prefecture villages (Vamos, Alikianos, Vrisses, Alikampos, Kournas — the Apokoronas belt) but this is not confirmed against primary text. Nothing found suggests it reaches either city's actual urban core

fact_label: Article 5C 50% income-tax break for new Greek tax residents
unit: % exemption on income tax, up to 7 years
notes: Requires not having been a Greek tax resident in 5 of the prior 6 years. At €60,000 gross income, brings effective rate to ~9.8% — a genuinely strong number relative to the Latin America research, weighs heavily for active/freelance income specifically

fact_label: Bureaucratic texture — genuinely slow, not exaggeration
value_raw: Strict, slow, prone to requirement changes and last-minute document demands even for successful applicants; AFM tax number a hard prerequisite for nearly everything
notes: Materially different bureaucratic texture than Guatemala's. Many expats hire a professional tax representative specifically to avoid this

fact_label: Closing costs (transfer tax + notary + registration + lawyer)
notes: Transfer tax ~3.09% (3% + municipal surcharge); notary 0.8-1.5%; registration ~0.5%; lawyer/conveyancing 1-2% or flat €2,500-5,000. **No foreign-buyer surcharge found** — applies identically to Greek and foreign buyers, a genuine structural plus vs. Portugal's confirmed 49-143% foreign-buyer premium

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Greek civil law very likely has its own proindiviso/condominio-equivalent and horizontal-property regime, given how common apartment-building ownership is — not confirmed by name this pass, a real gap in the now four-country (Guatemala/Colombia/Mexico/Argentina) pattern

value_raw: No national currency-devaluation or capital-control risk (ECB monetary policy, not a national lever); Moody's upgraded Greece to investment-grade Baa3, March 2026 — full return to investment-grade across all major agencies
notes: S&P, Fitch, DBRS, Scope all confirmed investment-grade with stable-to-positive outlooks by April 2026. Debt-to-GDP down ~50 points since 2020 peak (156.1% end-2024, projected 140.6% by late 2026)

fact_label: Decentralized Administration of Crete (residence-permit office) location
value_raw: Physically located in Heraklion — a real ~1.5-2.5hr drive for a Chania-based applicant
notes: Same shape of friction as Guatemala's Antigua-vs-Atitlán immigration office (the IGM) distance note — real but minor, doesn't change the underlying legal-substance picture

fact_label: Digital Nomad Visa accepts passive income as qualifying proof
value_raw: No
notes: Active remote-work income only — mirrors FIP's own boundary in the opposite direction

fact_label: Digital Nomad Visa application process change (2026)
value_raw: In-country applications abolished as of Feb 5, 2026 (Law 5275/2026) — must obtain from a Greek consulate/embassy abroad before travel
notes: Once issued, valid 2 years, renewable for successive 2-year periods provided income requirement continues to be met

fact_label: Digital Nomad Visa converts to permanent residency
value_raw: conditional-backstop: EU Long-Term Residence Directive 2003/109/EC
notes: Not a national fast lane — the Digital Nomad Visa (DNV) route itself has no dedicated conversion path; renewal years do count toward the EU-wide threshold via the named Directive. Root mechanism: EU Long-Term Residence Directive 2003/109/EC — a category-agnostic backstop, only 6 excluded categories (students, seasonal workers, au pairs, asylum seekers, temporary/subsidiary protection), the DNV route isn't one of them. Structurally an EU/Schengen feature, not a Greece-specific quirk. Not a Colombia-style dead-end Visitor category, but also no dedicated Greek fast lane.
Same figure as FIP's single-applicant threshold, but for active remote-work income, not passive

fact_label: Entry/Exit System (EES) / 183-day Digital Nomad Visa (DNV) renewal cross-check — Schengen infrastructure vs. Greek administrative practice
value_raw: The Entry/Exit System (EES) itself confirmed EU/Schengen-wide (all 29 states since 2026-04-10, Greece included); whether Greek immigration actually cross-checks EES data against Digital Nomad Visa (DNV) renewal (the way Portugal's AIMA is documented to do) is not yet confirmed
notes: Only generic, uncited aggregator-guide language found for the Greek-specific practice question — no Ministry of Migration and Asylum source, lawyer write-up, or lived account

fact_label: National air-ambulance service (EKAV) — free public island-to-mainland medevac
value_raw: Free of charge for a life-threatening condition, any of Greece's 150+ inhabited islands to a mainland public hospital, via the National Centre for Emergency Care (EKAV) helicopter/fixed-wing (dial 166/112); ~3,000 patient transports nationally, June 2021-March 2025
notes: snfghi.org, helis.com. A genuine national safety-net finding this project doesn't have documented at this level of specificity for most other candidates; distinct from the paid private air-ambulance tier (Athens Medevac, REVA, EMSOS, MEK JET) that private/expat insurance typically covers

fact_label: Emergency treatment obligation regardless of insurance status — billing outcome unresolved
value_raw: Public hospitals cannot refuse emergency treatment to anyone regardless of insurance/nationality; sources conflict on whether a non-EU patient is billed afterward or treated free the way a European Health Insurance Card (EHIC) holder is
notes: globalcitizensolutions.com says no charge; internationalinsurance.com/air-dr.com say non-EEA patients receive a bill/may be asked to pay upfront. Named as a real open conflict, not resolved by picking the more reassuring answer

fact_label: European Travel Information and Authorisation System (ETIAS) pre-travel authorization — coming, not yet live
value_raw: ~€20, expected Q4 2026, transitional grace period into ~April 2027
notes: Does not change the 90/180 ceiling itself — a pre-screening layer on top of the existing visa-exempt allowance

value_raw: Transparency/GPAI enforcement provisions take effect August 2, 2026 — only relevant if income model involves selling AI-adjacent services to EU-based clients
notes: Not confirmed as any specific income model — flagged for follow-up

fact_label: EU/EEA citizen public-healthcare access route (context, distinct from a non-EU visa holder's route)
value_raw: The European Health Insurance Card (EHIC) gives EU/EEA/Swiss citizens access to Greece's public system on the same terms as a Greek national, for a temporary stay; full integration on relocation
notes: eu-healthcare.eopyy.gov.gr. The genuinely different-shaped access route this criterion's definition specifically asked to be named precisely, relative to non-EU access above

fact_label: Financially Independent Person (FIP) visa income threshold
notes: ~2.8x Guatemala's Rentista threshold ($1,250/month). Cannot work in Greece under this permit — passive-income-only

fact_label: FIP visa accepts passive income as qualifying proof
value_raw: Yes
notes: Explicitly passive/retirement-style income — must spend 183+ days/year in Greece, which also triggers Greek tax residency

fact_label: FIP visa converts to permanent residency / EU long-term status
value_raw: Yes
notes: 5 years continuous residence (with A2 Greek) or 12 years without it, leads to EU long-term residence status. Citizenship available after 7 years. Time counts toward PR from day one — no Colombia-style non-counting Visitor category

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: No quantified foreign-buyer premium found (closing costs identical for both); no property-fraud pattern equivalent to Guatemala's or Morocco's melkia risk was specifically searched for

fact_label: Foreign-investment posture — Golden Visa maintained and expanded, thresholds rising
value_raw: Law 5275/2026 (eff. 2026-02-06) folded residence/work permits into one procedure and added new start-up/innovation investment routes; thresholds rose (not fell) across the board
notes: Welcome, but priced and regulated more tightly than five years ago," not "closing the door

fact_label: Gentrification backlash — organized but not foreigner/nomad-targeted
value_raw: Genuinely organized, peer-reviewed-documented protest movement in Chania (student unions, neighbourhood assemblies, 'Initiative Against Touristification') targeting the tourism/short-term-rental economy, not foreign residents or digital nomads as a group
notes: Zaimakis & Papadaki, *City* journal Vol. 29(3-4), July 2025 — peer-reviewed. A genuinely third pattern vs. CDMX (organized + foreigner-targeted) and Medellín (unorganized + foreigner-targeted): organized but tourism/Airbnb-targeted, not foreigner-targeted

value_raw: No
notes: Grants a 5-year renewable residency permit with no minimum-stay trigger — unlike the Financially Independent Person (FIP) or Digital Nomad Visa (DNV) routes' 183-day tax-residency trigger
Crete sits in the standard tier, not the €800,000 tier (Attica/Athens, Thessaloniki, Mykonos, Santorini, islands >3,100 population). Minimum 120m², single property only

value_raw: Yes
notes: Golden Visa properties cannot be used for Airbnb-style rental — violating risks permit cancellation plus a €50,000 fine. A real constraint given how central short-term-rental income is to Crete's property economics

fact_label: Illegal-entry penalty (distinct regime, not ordinary overstay)
value_raw: Up to €10,000 fine for illegal entry, €30,000 for re-entry after a prior rejection; potential prison terms
notes: infomigrants.net, Greece's Migration Code (Law 4251/2014). Applies to irregular border crossing, not to a visa-compliant tourist who simply overstays

fact_label: Institutional-trust erosion — farm-subsidy fraud scandal and Tempi rail-disaster aftermath
value_raw: Farm-subsidy fraud described as Mitsotakis's 'biggest crisis since taking office'; 2023 Tempi rail disaster aftermath still a live grievance — 80%+ of Greeks polled Jan 2026 don't trust the justice system to uncover the truth
notes: European Conservative (2026); GreekReporter (2025-12). A credible emergent political figure (Maria Karystianou, victim's-family advocate) polls at 31.8% potential support, didn't exist a year ago

value_raw: First criminal convictions 2026-02-26 — an Athens court sentenced four Intellexa-linked individuals (suspended pending appeal); opposition calling it evidence of 'a deep state set up by the prime minister'
notes: Amnesty International, HRW, TechCrunch, OCCRP, all 2026-02/03

value_raw: No
notes: Next expected 2027. PM Mitsotakis/New Democracy remain dominant in polling (~31%, 2026-05) largely because the opposition is fragmented across ~12 parties, not because public trust is high

value_raw: No National Organization for Health Care Services (EOPYY)/public-system coverage as a function of holding FIP, Digital Nomad Visa, or Golden Visa; private insurance is a hard application requirement for FIP and Golden Visa specifically
notes: feather-insurance.com, movingto.com, globalcitizensolutions.com. A genuine, unresolved sourcing conflict found the same pass (whether AMKA registration alone unlocks EOPYY post-residency) — flagged as unconfirmed, not disproven; contribution-based EOPYY eligibility has no obvious trigger for a passive-income/remote-work visa holder with no Greek employer

value_raw: Yes
notes: Both cities lose direct international flight service outside ~late April-October, forcing an Athens connection; no regular winter flight between the two Cretan cities themselves

value_raw: Confirmed, not a [GAP]: the EU's core pet-import mechanics (microchip, rabies vaccination timing, titer-test-if-unlisted, Animal Health Certificate, no quarantine if compliant) apply identically to dogs, cats, and ferrets — all three are treated uniformly as the species covered by Annex I, Part A of the governing regulation. The one real, sourced divergence found: the EU's Echinococcus multilocularis (tapeworm) treatment requirement (praziquantel or equivalent, 24-120 hours before entry) applies to dogs ONLY — cats and ferrets are explicitly exempt — but this treatment is itself only required by a named short list of destination states (Finland, Ireland, Malta, UK via Northern Ireland, Norway), and Greece is not one of them, so the divergence doesn't actually change anything for a cat or dog entering Crete specifically
notes: food.ec.europa.eu (EC's own official pages), fetched directly 2026-07-14 — first candidate in this project to close the standing cat/dog-divergence [GAP] flagged as unresearched project-wide. The other real dog-vs-cat divergence, breed restrictions, has no cat equivalent structurally but Greece's own dangerous-breed list is itself unresolved from this pass (see the separate breed-restriction row below)

fact_label: Pet import (dog) — dangerous-breed restriction status, unresolved
value_raw: Genuinely unresolved from desk research: multiple aggregator sources name American Pit Bull Terrier, American Staffordshire Terrier, Dogo Argentino, Japanese Tosa, and Fila Brasileiro as restricted/banned in Greece, tied to Law 4830/2021, but no primary Greek government source confirms this, and the sourcing self-contradicts (one source states in the same breath that Greece does not publish a banned-breed list, then names one)
notes: Greek Ministry of Rural Development and Food (minagric.gr) and Greek MFA (mfa.gr) both returned 403 on direct fetch this pass — a real, named primary-source gap, not silently skipped. This is dog-specific with structurally no cat equivalent (breed-restriction regimes are dog-only worldwide), not an unresearched species gap

fact_label: Pet import (dog) — rabies titer / quarantine requirement
value_raw: No titer test and no quarantine for a dog departing from a listed third country (US, UK, Canada, and Australia all confirmed listed) with a compliant microchip + rabies vaccination (12+ weeks old, 21-day wait) + Animal Health Certificate (valid 10 days). Departing from an unlisted country instead requires a titer test — blood drawn 30+ days after primary vaccination, tested at a designated lab, certificate not issuable until 90+ days after the sample was drawn. No quarantine either way once documentation is complete and correctly sequenced; incomplete/incorrect paperwork can get a pet held or refused at the Border Inspection Post instead
notes: food.ec.europa.eu (EC's own non-EU pet-entry page), fetched directly 2026-07-14; cross-checked against petrelocation.com/pettravel.com for the Greece-specific no-quarantine-if-compliant confirmation. Animal Health Certificate must be typed (not handwritten) and produced in both English and Greek — a real practical-friction finding, sourced to relocation-industry content rather than a Greek government page directly

fact_label: Pet import — governing EU regulatory instrument (2026 update)
value_raw: As of 22 April 2026, non-commercial pet movement into/within the EU runs on Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2026/131 and Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/705, superseding the 2013-era regulation numbers (576/2013 / 577/2013) most existing pet-relocation literature still cites
notes: food.ec.europa.eu, fetched directly 2026-07-14. Core mechanics (microchip, vaccination timing, titer test for unlisted countries, no quarantine if compliant) carry over from the prior regulation largely intact; two real tightenings: mandatory microchip scan-and-verify at every EU entry point (replacing spot checks), and the EU pet passport's EU-residents-only rule is now explicit in the current text itself

value_raw: Yes
notes: Pickpocketing (old-town streets, ports, buses, beaches) and tourist-facing scams (taxi overcharging, fake tour guides, inflated restaurant charges), concentrated in busy tourist areas. Violent crime genuinely rare — Crete consistently rated among Greece's safer regions

fact_label: Private health insurance — age-gated enrollment ceiling
value_raw: New-policy issuance commonly stops at 65 (all-hospital-access plans); a second, network-limited tier commonly caps new enrollment at 80. Once active, coverage continues regardless of age — the ceiling gates starting a policy, not staying on one
notes: feather-insurance.com, cross-referenced against justlanded.com and nestia.gr. A genuine compound gate specifically for Golden Visa/FIP applicants, since both require proof of private coverage as part of the application itself — age-neutral fact, not scored against any one reader's own age

fact_label: Private health insurance — monthly premium by age (local plans)
unit: €/month (≈€80 at age 30, ≈€120-180 at 55, ≈€200-300 at 65; international/global plans start meaningfully higher at each age tier)
notes: pacificprime.com, expatden.com. Budget local plans start ~€30/month; premium local plans run €210+/month, independent of the age-tier figures

value_raw: universal-locality-permit
notes: A separate permit requirement, applying to everyone (not just foreigners), tied to statute-enumerated place-names — the opposite move from every other shape's foreigner-vs-citizen logic.

value_raw: open-freehold
notes: Crete absent from Law 1892/1990 Art. 24-27's exhaustive restricted-area list, two independent professional sources.

value_raw: universal-locality-permit
notes: Flagged tension: source's literal text reads 'restricts property purchase to Greek nationals only near Souda' — closer to Shape D's logic than Shape E's — kept this project's ratified Shape-E classification rather than re-litigate; discrepancy worth a primary-text read.

fact_label: Restricted border-zone list (Law 1892/1990, Art. 24-27) — does Crete appear
value_raw: No
notes: Two independent professional sources (Karagiannis-Stamatiou law firm; Christos Terzidis, Notary of Komotini/President of the Thrace Notary Association, hosted by the Thessaloniki Notary Association) both confirm Crete is absent from the exhaustive enumerated restricted-area list. Raised from "leans resolved, Medium" to Confirmed Medium-High this pass — short of High only because no lawyer engaged for an actual purchase has confirmed it

value_raw: Never a legitimate workaround on paper (area-wide, not per-country count); the practical gap from manual stamping is now closed by the Entry/Exit System (EES), not just tightened
notes: The Entry/Exit System (EES) reached full operational status across all 29 Schengen states 2026-04-10, Greece included. ~7,000 overstayers caught in EES's first six months per the EU's own "State of Schengen" report (released week of 2026-05-20)

value_raw: 90 days within any rolling 180-day period, area-wide, not per-country
notes: European Commission Migration and Home Affairs, Annex II of Regulation (EU) 2018/1806. Visa-exempt tier includes US/UK/Canada/Australia/NZ/Japan/S.Korea/Singapore/Brazil/EU-EEA-Switzerland; a visa-required tier (Annex I) exists but is capped at the same 90/180 once issued

unit: earthquakes/year average (26-year data, in or near Crete)
notes: Greece is the most seismically active country in Europe. At least 4 magnitude-7+ events since 1900 (~once every 30-35 years). 2025: 477 M4+ quakes within 300km of Crete (strongest M6.2), including a May 2025 M6.1 south of Crete serious enough to trigger a tsunami warning, and a shallow M5.7 near Heraklion April 2026

value_raw: Yes
notes: Confirmed real and current (2018 Presidential Decree, not historical curiosity) via two independent sources (Ledaki/Broosco; PD 44/2018 itself). Exact footprint/scope unconfirmed — restricts property purchase to Greek nationals only near Souda, but whether it reaches beyond the former Kalamion prisons/naval-base perimeter itself is not confirmed

value_raw: 183+ days/year in Greece
notes: Worldwide income becomes taxable once triggered — same pattern as Mexico, opposite of Guatemala's territorial system. Article 5C break mitigates but doesn't erase this, and expires after 7 years

value_raw: Yes
legitimate

unit: € (tiered: <30 days over ~€500, 30-90 days ~€1,200, 90+ days up to €1,500)
notes: greecetravel.com/mazarakis — a lived-account-adjacent source, not law-firm-grade. Plus a 5-year national blacklist barring re-entry. Broader Schengen-wide aggregator figures (€300-5,000+) sit around this narrower Greek-specific schedule, not confirmed against a primary government citation

value_raw: No routine extension — only exceptional circumstances (force majeure, humanitarian grounds, or a fee-bearing 'serious personal reasons' category, €30), and any extension still cannot exceed 90/180 total
notes: axa-schengen.com. Honest bottom line per the file itself: for a normal tourist stay with no emergency, 90/180 is a hard ceiling

fact_label: Tourist time counts toward FIP, Digital Nomad Visa (DNV), or Golden Visa clocks
value_raw: No
notes: All three long-term routes require an actual issued residence permit; prior visiting time does not pre-load any of their clocks. Structural note: the 90/180 tourist ceiling sits well below the 183-day tax-residency trigger, so a compliant tourist cannot accidentally trigger tax residency on tourist status alone
No foreign-buyer surcharge found — applies identically to Greek and foreign buyers.

value_raw: Yes
notes: Achlia wildfire (eastern Crete, July 2, 2025) forced ~5,000 evacuations. European Drought Observatory recorded rising alert-level drought conditions in Crete as of late March 2026; warm/dry winter increasing dead-fuel availability
